tracks.meaning: to watch over or protect someone or something carefully
the ridge of skin on the inner side of the wrist or forearm
a defensive player in hockey or lacrosse positioned between the forwards and goaltender
a person who supervises or protects another, especially a minor or their property
a soldier assigned to watch over a place or person
a mental or emotional barrier that protects someone from harm or unwanted influence
a metal or leather device worn to protect the hand and wrist while fencing
a cautious or defensive attitude adopted to protect oneself
in boxing, a warning given to a fighter for breaking the rules
